result.summary: Sequel exposes a documented REST API for event, company, networking, customization, and usage operations, plus a Node.js SDK package that wraps the same API; both use Sequel-issued client credentials and bearer-token auth.
result.description: Sequel provides webinar, live session, and virtual event components for embedding real-time video, networking, registration, and analytics into other products. It is used by companies to run and measure live and on-demand event experiences on their own websites or platforms.

sequel_oauth_client.label: Sequel API client credentials (Client ID + Client Secret)
sequel_oauth_client.setup: Create a Sequel account via [Sequel Admin sign-up](https://admin.sequel.io/sign-up), then in the Sequel Admin dashboard open the Integrations/Marketplace area and find the **Sequel API** integration card. Copy the `client_id` and `client_secret` shown there. The help article [Company ID for API Workflows with Sequel.io](https://help.sequel.io/en/articles/8843778-company-id-for-api-workflows-with-sequel-io) describes opening Marketplace → Configure under the Sequel API card; the API docs [Getting Started with Sequel](https://docs.introvoke.com/docs/getting-started) say to copy the Client ID and Client Secret from the Integrations page in the admin dashboard.

sequel_access_token.label: Sequel API bearer access token
sequel_access_token.setup: First obtain Sequel API client credentials from the Sequel admin dashboard. Then `POST` JSON to [`https://api.introvoke.com/api/oauth/token`](https://api.introvoke.com/api/oauth/token) with `client_id`, `client_secret`, `audience`=`https://www.introvoke.com/api`, and `grant_type`=`client_credentials` as shown in [Getting Started with Sequel](https://docs.introvoke.com/docs/getting-started). Use the returned `access_token` as a `Bearer` token when calling the API.

mechanics.command: Configure the SDK with `sequel.configure({ clientId, clientSecret })`
